title: Production Change Management Audit
summary: Assess whether production changes are approved, tested, segregated, and reviewed after deployment.
objective: Assess whether production application and infrastructure changes are formally approved, tested, segregated, and implemented with adequate evidence of governance.
scope: Segregation of duties for developers and deployers

controls.name: Approved Production Changes
controls.description: All production changes should have documented approval before implementation.

controls.name: Pre-Implementation Testing Evidence
controls.description: Production deployments should include evidence of successful testing before release.

controls.name: Segregation of Development and Deployment
controls.description: Developers should not directly deploy unreviewed changes into production without compensating control.

controls.name: Post-Incident Review of Failed Changes
controls.description: Failed or rolled-back changes should have a documented post-implementation review.

evidence.title: Emergency Change Log
evidence.content: Emergency deployment EC-104 restored service after a billing outage, but the approval field is blank and no CAB follow-up date is listed.

evidence.title: Release Ticket CHG-8821
evidence.content: The release ticket for the customer portal upgrade includes implementation steps and rollback notes, but the testing attachment section is empty.

evidence.title: Production Access Matrix
evidence.content: The production access matrix shows three developers retain direct deploy rights in the live Kubernetes environment.

evidence.title: Pipeline Deployment Screenshot
evidence.content: Pipeline history shows a production deployment triggered manually by developer account g.sinyangwe without a linked change ticket reference.

evidence.title: Incident Review Minutes
evidence.content: Minutes from the payment outage call note that the rollback succeeded, but the team deferred root-cause review until next sprint and never documented it.

evidence.title: Change Management Standard
evidence.content: The standard requires approval, documented testing, restricted deployment access, and post-implementation review for failed changes.

issues.title: Emergency Change Missing Approval
issues.description: An emergency production change was implemented without documented approval or retrospective CAB review.
issues.recommendation: Require formal emergency approval capture and retrospective CAB review for urgent production changes.

issues.title: Production Release Without Testing Evidence
issues.description: A production release lacked documented evidence that required testing had been completed before deployment.
issues.recommendation: Block production release progression until testing evidence is attached to the change record.

issues.title: Developers Retain Direct Production Deployment Access
issues.description: Developer access to production deployments weakens segregation of duties over code promotion.
issues.recommendation: Restrict direct production deployment rights and route releases through controlled service accounts or release operators.

issues.title: Failed Change Lacked Post-Implementation Review
issues.description: A failed production change was rolled back without a documented post-incident review or root-cause analysis.
issues.recommendation: Require documented post-implementation review for rolled-back or failed production releases.

inbox.subject: Kickoff: Production Change Management
inbox.preview: Determine whether change control is real or just paperwork around releases.
inbox.body: Focus on approvals, testing evidence, segregation of duties, and whether failed changes are truly learned from. The question is whether production releases are controlled in practice.

inbox.subject: Release records attached
inbox.preview: Sending the recent change log, release ticket, and the current standard.
inbox.body: Most releases follow the standard path, though we did move quickly during last month's billing incident. Happy to explain the release workflow if helpful.

interviewPrompts.question: Can developers deploy directly into production?
interviewPrompts.answer: A few senior engineers still can, mainly for hotfix speed. We planned to remove that after pipeline hardening but it is still in place.

interviewPrompts.question: Why is there no testing evidence attached to CHG-8821?
interviewPrompts.answer: The team said testing happened in staging, but they did not upload screenshots or signoff before the release window opened.

interviewPrompts.question: What happened with emergency change EC-104?
interviewPrompts.answer: We were restoring service and skipped the approval capture at the time. We intended to log retrospective CAB review later, but it never got added.

interviewPrompts.question: Was the portal deployment triggered through the controlled release path?
interviewPrompts.answer: No, I manually triggered it from the pipeline because the release coordinator was offline and the fix was urgent.

interviewPrompts.question: Was a postmortem completed after the payment outage rollback?
interviewPrompts.answer: Operations captured the outage notes, but the formal post-incident review never happened because the team moved to the next release cycle.

closeout.managementMessage: The engagement indicates release activity is moving quickly, but governance over approvals, testing, and follow-up is inconsistent. Production change control needs stronger enforcement if management wants confidence in release quality.
closeout.auditorReflection: Change audits reward attention to the release trail. The strongest conclusions come from matching who deployed, what evidence existed before deployment, and what happened after things went wrong.
